Happy people are those whose land is filled with the knowledge of God! Happy people who have their hearts filled with that knowledge! It is the glory and happiness of a people to have God among them by his ordinances. When enemies of the church act proudly, it will appear that God is above them. See the power of God's rebukes. Christians may apply this with pleasure to the advantages bestowed by the Redeemer.

God’s people are the meek of the earth, the quiet in the land, who suffer wrong but do none. The righteous God seems to keep silence for a long time, yet he will eventually make judgment heard. We live in an angry and provoking world; we often feel much, and are likely to fear more from the wrath of man. What will not turn to his praise shall not be allowed to break out. He can set bounds to the wrath of man, as he does to the raging sea; it shall come up to this point, and no further. Let all submit to God. Our prayers and praises, and especially our hearts, are the gifts we should bring to the Lord. His name is glorious, and he is the proper object of our fear. He will cut off the spirit of princes; he will slip it off easily, as we slip off a flower from the stalk or a bunch of grapes from the vine; so the word signifies. He can discourage even the most daring: since there is no contending with God, it is our wisdom and our duty to submit to him. Let us seek his favor as our portion, and commit all our concerns to him.
